title: linaria fallax
intro: Linaria fallax, also known as Toadflax or Butter and Eggs, is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic and to help reduce fever. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its possible use in reducing inflammation and promoting digestion. In herbal preparations, Linaria fallax is often used as a tea, tincture, or infusion, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
